# Frontend Architecture

## Source vs. generated files

TypeScript under `src/` is the source of truth for browser behavior. The previous `assets/ts` and `assets/js` mirror was generated by `tsconfig.json`: `assets/ts` was the root directory and `assets/js` was the compiler output. That meant source and generated JavaScript lived side by side.

The project now keeps generated JavaScript out of source tracking:

- Edit source in `src/`.
- Run `npm run build:ts` to emit browser scripts into `assets/js/`.
- Do not edit files in `assets/js/`; they are ignored generated output.
- Treat `bundle.js` and `assets/css/tailwind.css` the same way: generated by npm scripts, ignored by Git, and rebuilt before publishing.
- Run `npm run build` before serving or publishing so `bundle.js`, `assets/css/tailwind.css`, and `assets/js/**` are current.
- Tests should target `src/` directly. Integration tests that need browser-global scripts can transpile TypeScript from `src/` during the test run instead of requiring committed `assets/js/**` files.

## Directory responsibilities

```text
src/
  app/       bootstrap and router orchestration
  core/      shared DOM, metadata, theme, animation, Material, and type utilities
  features/  user-facing feature modules grouped by feature and layer
assets/
  css/       global CSS, tokens, and Tailwind input; Tailwind output is generated
  data/      static JSON loaded by the app
  icons/     PWA and favicon assets
  images/    static images and illustrations
pages/       HTML fragments loaded by the SPA router
```

## App layer

`src/app/main.ts` wires DOM readiness, theme, drawer setup, route interception, and initial route loading. Router support code lives in `src/app/router/`:

- `routes.ts` registers route IDs, fragment paths, titles, metadata, and page load hooks.
- `Router.ts` normalizes route IDs, loads route markup, swaps content, updates active navigation state, and invokes page handlers.
- `contentLoader.ts` fetches HTML fragments from `pages/`.
- `history.ts` updates the document title and hash history.
- `animation.ts` isolates route transition helpers.

## Core layer

Use `src/core/` for cross-feature code only:

- `dom/` contains shared DOM helpers and loading overlay utilities.
- `metadata/` owns route metadata tag updates.
- `theme/` owns theme selection.
- `animations/` owns shared animation utilities reused across routes and features.
- `material/` registers Material Web components for the Rollup bundle.
- `types/` contains shared TypeScript interfaces and global declarations.

If code belongs to one feature, keep it in that feature instead of putting it in core.

## Feature layer

Feature modules are grouped first by feature, then by layer:

- `data/` fetches or reads external/static data and normalizes transport concerns.
- `domain/` holds feature-specific mappers, models, and rules that are not tied to the DOM.
- `presentation/` owns DOM rendering, event wiring, and page/component behavior.

Current feature examples:

- `features/navigation-drawer/presentation/`
- `features/blog/data/` and `features/blog/presentation/`
- `features/projects/presentation/`
- `features/resume/data/` and `features/resume/presentation/`
- `features/songs/data/`, `features/songs/domain/`, and `features/songs/presentation/`
- `features/faq/data/` and `features/faq/presentation/`
- `features/contact/presentation/`
- `features/apps/smart-cleaner/presentation/`

Add new page-specific behavior under the matching feature folder. Create `data/`, `domain/`, or `presentation/` only when that layer has real responsibility for the feature.

## Routing flow

1. `index.html` loads Material Web from `bundle.js`; `index.ts` delegates Material registration to `src/core/material/registerMaterial.ts`. It then loads generated feature/app scripts from `assets/js/**`.
2. `src/app/main.ts` captures the initial home markup and initializes the router.
3. Route links with hash fragments are intercepted and passed to `loadPageContent`.
4. `Router.ts` asks `RouterContentLoader` for the registered route content.
5. The router updates the page container, title, metadata, active drawer item, and page-specific ready hook.

Keep existing route IDs and `pages/**` paths stable unless a migration explicitly updates every caller and public URL.

## Styling rules

- Keep design tokens in `assets/css/variables.css`.
- Keep reset and base element rules in `assets/css/base.css`.
- Keep shared reusable component classes in `assets/css/components.css`.
- Keep page-specific styling in clearly named page files such as `assets/css/pages.css` or `assets/css/resume.css` until a future CSS split is introduced.
- Prefer Material/Web component defaults over custom CSS.
- Do not style component internals globally unless the file includes a comment explaining the exception and the component behavior being preserved.

## Adding a page or feature

1. Add static HTML under `pages/` if the router will load a fragment.
2. Register the route in `src/app/router/routes.ts`, including metadata.
3. Add feature behavior under `src/features/<feature>/...`, using `data/`, `domain/`, and `presentation/` according to responsibility.
4. For app-specific pages, add behavior under `src/features/apps/<app-name>/...` and keep legal/static HTML content in `pages/drawer/more/apps/` unless a route migration requires otherwise.
5. Add any shared helper only if at least two features need it.
6. Add or update tests under `__tests__/`, importing TypeScript from `src/` instead of generated `assets/js/`.
7. Run `npm test -- --runInBand`, `npm run build:ts`, `npm run build:css`, and `npm run build`.

## Static assets

Keep SEO and PWA files at their existing public URLs:

- `robots.txt`
- `sitemap.xml`
- `assets/manifest.json`
- `assets/icons/**`
- `app-ads.txt`

Static JSON belongs in `assets/data/`. Images and illustrations belong in `assets/images/`. Icons belong in `assets/icons/` unless a hosting migration introduces a dedicated `public/` copy step.

## GitHub Pages deployment

The repository includes a GitHub Pages workflow at `.github/workflows/pages.yml`. It installs dependencies, runs the Jest suite against `src/`, runs `npm run deploy`, and uploads a prepared `_site/` artifact after `bundle.js`, `assets/css/tailwind.css`, and `assets/js/**` have been regenerated. This keeps generated output out of Git while still publishing a complete static site.
